findings.title: Slab allocator UAF exploitable through vtable hijack but privileged callback unreachable
findings.statement: The custom slab allocator metadata layout was reversed via Ghidra decompilation and confirmed with a GDB Python free-list walker. Breakpoints on the allocator fast-path show that after a controlled free sequence, a fake object can be placed at a predictable slab offset. GDB single-stepping confirms the vtable dispatch redirects to the attacker-controlled pointer. However, reaching the privileged callback requires a second info leak (ASLR base of the callback table) that static analysis and the current heap state do not provide.

evidence.summary: debug_gdb_script: Python free-list walker confirms slab metadata layout and controlled re-allocation after UAF
debug_gdb_commands: breakpoints on allocator fast-path confirm fake object at target slab offset; single-step shows vtable redirect
